data.narrative:
Forterra is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Alberto Lacaze that has raised $228M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparables (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector, with five key private benchmarks including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two valuation methods triangulate a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $228M raised proxy, implying $433M. Peer-median benchmarking across the 6 same-stage, same-sector private comparables yields a $1.2B median valuation, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. This cohort includes Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B and Castelion and Defense Unicorns at $1.0B.

The triangulated valuation range is $368M (floor) to $1.4B (ceiling), with a median of $817M. The wide spread reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Forterra's commercial traction and technology positioning align with higher-quartile peers or closer to the lower-bound cohort. Key variables include revenue run rate, customer concentration, and defensibility relative to the named comparables. The peer-median method suggests upside to current fundraising multiples, but stage-progression produces a more conservative anchor.
